stories.headline: After Australia, UK bans rapper Kanye West from entering country: ‘Celebration of Nazism’ row, explained
stories.summary: The UK has banned US rapper Kanye West from entering the country due to his past antisemitic comments and celebration of Nazism. The decision was made as West was set to headline London's Wireless Festival in July. West has been criticized for voicing admiration for Adolf Hitler and releasing a song called "Heil Hitler" last year. He had also advertised a swastika T-shirt for sale on his website. Despite apologizing for his behavior in January and offering to meet with the UK's Jewish community, the UK government blocked his entry. The ban follows a similar decision by Australia, which cancelled his visa last May. The move has been supported by Liberal Democrat leader Sir Ed Davey, while Nigel Farage had warned against banning West, but described his comments as "Nazism". The Wireless Festival organizers now need to fill three headline slots at short notice.

stories.headline: Trump says 'whole civilization will die' in Iran if ultimatum expires
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has warned that "a whole civilization will die" in Iran if the country does not accept his ultimatum by the deadline. The ultimatum, which expires at 10am AEST, demands that Iran agree to a deal to end the conflict and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Trump has threatened to target Iranian infrastructure, including power stations and bridges, if the deadline is not met. He has stated that US bombing would destroy all of Iran's power stations and bridges within four hours of the deadline passing. Iran has responded by saying the conflict will continue until the enemy's surrender. The US president's comments have raised concerns that he may be prepared to commit serious war crimes by targeting the country's population. The situation remains tense as the deadline approaches.

stories.headline: U.S., Israel and Iran Accelerate Strikes Ahead of Trump’s Deadline
stories.summary: The United States and Israel have intensified strikes against Iran, targeting military installations and infrastructure. Israel struck eight road and rail bridges in Iran, while the US attacked military installations on Kharg Island in the Persian Gulf. Iran retaliated, but the US and Israel continued their assault, with the US hitting Kharg Island again. The escalation comes ahead of a deadline set by US President Donald Trump, with US officials expressing confidence that Iran will respond. The strikes aim to force Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial waterway for global oil trade. The US and Israel have coordinated their efforts, with American and Israeli officials confirming the joint operation. The situation remains tense, with further retaliation expected from Iran. The US and Israel's actions are seen as an attempt to exert pressure on Iran.

stories.headline: Marjorie Taylor Greene, Ro Khanna, Schumer call for 25th Amendment to remove Trump: 'Not a single bomb has...'
stories.summary: Former Representative Marjorie Taylor Greene called for the 25th Amendment to be invoked to remove President Donald Trump from office. Greene, a former strong ally of the president, made the statement after a public disagreement with Trump. She described his recent rhetoric as "evil and madness," citing the potential for harm to an entire civilization. Greene's comments came after Trump made a post that sparked concern, with Greene emphasizing that "not a single bomb has dropped on America" and advocating for his removal. Democratic Senator Chuck Schumer and Representative Ro Khanna also joined in calling for the 25th Amendment to be invoked, expressing similar concerns about Trump's actions. The White House has been reached out to for comment on the matter.

stories.headline: New video reveals iPhone 18 Pro Max, iPhone Fold dummy models Today brought a first-look photo at dummy models for iPhone Fold, iPhone 18 Pro, and iPhone 18 Pro Max—and now there’s a video too.
stories.summary: A new video showcases dummy models for Apple's iPhone Fold, iPhone 18 Pro, and iPhone 18 Pro Max. The iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max appear largely similar to their predecessors, with the exception of a smaller Dynamic Island. The foldable iPhone, potentially named iPhone Ultra, features a passport-style design, unlike any previous iPhone. The dummy models provide a first look at the design of these upcoming flagship phones. The iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max have minimal design changes, with slightly smaller camera and depth sensors on the front. The video, shared by leaker Sonny Dickson, offers a side-by-side comparison of the devices, giving a glimpse into the potential design of Apple's 2026 lineup, including the iPhone 18 Pro Max and the foldable iPhone. The designs are subject to change as they are based on dummy models.

stories.headline: Scentian Bio raises $7m to take its AI and insect “Imagine if you could detect a disease on your breath simply by blowing onto your phone,” Dr Andrew Kralicek says.
stories.summary: Scentian Bio has raised $7 million to advance its AI and insect-based technology for detecting and interpreting smells. The company's system utilizes insect smell receptors to detect volatile organic compounds (VOCs) and can identify smells that no insect would normally encounter. Initially focusing on the food industry, Scentian Bio is working with five global food companies on pilots and has a grant from the Gates Foundation to develop a prototype for detecting infectious diseases such as tuberculosis and malaria. The technology has various use cases, including food quality control, disease diagnostics, and environmental monitoring. Scentian Bio's sensors can also aid in determining the ideal harvest time for crops like kiwifruit, and the company is exploring pest detection and wellness monitoring applications. The startup is headquartered in Auckland and has an 18% stake held by the New Zealand Institute for Plant and Food.

stories.headline: Google Chrome's vertical tabs and full page Reading Mode are rolling out widely Finally out of beta
stories.summary: Google Chrome is rolling out two new features to streamline the browser and boost productivity. Vertical tabs are now available, allowing users to organize their tabs in a vertical layout, and a full-page Reading Mode has been introduced to provide a cleaner reading experience. Both features are being rolled out widely, having previously been in beta. To access the new features, users must be running the latest version of Chrome. The update aims to help manage cluttered browsing and improve user experience. The rollout is global, and users worldwide can now try out the vertical tabs feature without needing to enable flags. The new features are part of Google's efforts to enhance the Chrome browser and provide a more efficient and immersive browsing experience.

stories.headline: This Browser-Based Tool Simplifies Everyday PDF Workflows Thanks to this mega-deal, you can edit, convert, and sign files without installing software or relying on cloud uploads, all on the cheap!
stories.summary: A browser-based tool, PDFToolkit, simplifies everyday PDF workflows by allowing users to edit, convert, and sign files without installing software or relying on cloud uploads. This tool brings all PDF management features into one place for a flat fee of $79. With PDFToolkit, users can modify texts and images, merge and split files, add e-signatures, and convert files into other formats. The tool uses AI-powered technology to streamline the PDF process. It also allows users to work with PDF files directly in their web browser from any device, creating, editing, organizing, converting, merging, or e-signing files as needed. This reflects the shift towards cloud-based work, enabling common PDF tasks to be completed directly in a web browser without the need for local software. The tool provides a comprehensive suite of features similar to Acrobat.

stories.headline: Stephon Castle Reacts to Victor Wembanyama Injury in Spurs-76ers
stories.summary: San Antonio Spurs guard Stephon Castle provided an update on Victor Wembanyama's condition after the team's win over the Philadelphia 76ers. Wembanyama suffered a rib contusion during the game, prompting concern among Spurs fans. However, Castle shared a positive update, indicating that Wembanyama is "looking good" postgame. The Spurs secured their 60th win of the season despite Wembanyama's early exit, with Castle leading the team to victory with a triple-double. The win was a total team effort, and the Spurs appear to have dodged serious injury concerns regarding their superstar Wembanyama. The rib contusion is not expected to be a long-term issue, but the team's caution is likely a precautionary measure to ensure Wembanyama's health.

stories.headline: The Spanish Football Federation sanctions Piqué
stories.summary: The Spanish Football Federation's Disciplinary Committee sanctioned Gerard Piqué, the former Barcelona defender, for hurling insults and threats at a referee after a match. The incident occurred when Piqué headed to the stadium tunnel and verbally abused the official, according to the referee's report. The sanction carries potential fines ranging from 3,000 to 30,000 euros, depending on the severity of the offense. This incident is separate from Piqué's involvement in a trial related to the Spanish Super Cup being hosted in Saudi Arabia, where he denied receiving payments from the Royal Spanish Football Federation. Piqué had acted as an intermediary between Saudi Arabia and the RFEF in a deal worth €40 million annually. The federation has not released an official statement on the incident, but the disciplinary action has been taken against the former player.

stories.headline: 2026 College Basketball Transfer Portal: Intel on Flory Bidunga, Somto Cyril Acaden Lewis, Juke Harris
stories.summary: Juke Harris, Wake Forest's leading scorer, has entered the college basketball transfer portal after averaging 21.4 points, 6.5 rebounds, and 1.9 assists per game in his sophomore season. He is among several notable players making the move, including Kansas big man Flory Bidunga and Saint Mary's Paulius Murauskas. Somto Cyril and Acaden Lewis, from Georgia and Villanova respectively, are also exploring their options. The transfer portal is open for 15 days, allowing Division I men's basketball players to seek new opportunities. Other players, such as Aiden Sherrell, Bryson Tiller, and Elmarko Jackson, are also expected to enter the portal, marking the start of a busy period for college basketball coaches and programs looking to reshape their rosters. The portal's opening has sparked significant interest and activity in the college basketball world.

stories.headline: Thuney to be Installed in Football Ring of Honor
stories.summary: Joe Thuney will be inducted into NC State Football's Ring of Honor, as announced by Director of Athletics Boo Corrigan and head coach Dave Doeren. Thuney played college football for the NC State Wolfpack before being selected by the New England Patriots in the 2016 NFL draft. He made history as the first player to start in the Super Bowl in each of his first three seasons. Thuney has had a successful NFL career, recently signing a two-year, $35 million contract extension with the Bears. He also filled in at left tackle for the Chiefs' playoff run, earning folk-hero status despite it not being his natural position. Thuney's accolades continue to grow, with NFL.com making a case for him as a frontrunner for the league's newest individual honor, backed by data on his performance.

stories.headline: Women cannot be seen as ‘untouchables’ for three days a month: Justice Nagarathna in Sabarimala case
stories.summary: Supreme Court judge BV Nagarathna questioned the notion of treating women as "untouchables" for three days a month during hearings on discrimination against women at religious places, including the Sabarimala temple. She emphasized that a woman cannot be considered untouchable for three days and then cease to be so on the fourth day. Justice Nagarathna made this remark while addressing the Union government, highlighting the inconsistency in applying Article 17, which abolishes untouchability. The comment came during a review of the 2018 Sabarimala judgment, which ruled against age restrictions on women's entry into the temple. The nine-judge bench, including Justice Nagarathna as the sole woman, is examining constitutional questions related to the case. Nagarathna's statement underscores the issue of gender-based discrimination and the application of anti-untouchability laws in religious contexts.

stories.headline: Not Reviewing Sabarimala Verdict In Reference; Only Considering Constitutional Questions, Says Supreme Court
stories.summary: The Supreme Court is hearing review petitions against its 2018 verdict on Sabarimala, which allowed women of menstruating age to enter the temple. A nine-judge Constitution bench is examining the petitions, but clarified it is not revisiting the earlier judgment directly. Instead, the court is considering broader constitutional questions, such as whether religious practices can justify discrimination. The bench held that religious practices must conform to constitutional morality. The 2018 verdict had struck down the ban on women of menstruating age from entering Sabarimala, citing discrimination. The court will begin hearing arguments on the constitutional questions, aiming to settle key issues related to religious freedom and gender equality. The petitions challenge the landmark judgment, which was seen as a significant step towards gender equality in Hindu religious practices.

stories.headline: NCERT reconstitutes textbook committee after Supreme Court rap
stories.summary: The National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT) has reconstituted its textbook committee after a Supreme Court order. The move comes after the court's ban on a Class 8 social science textbook that discussed judicial corruption. The committee was first constituted in July 2020, but has been reformed nearly three years later through an NCERT notification issued last week. The new committee includes Amarendra Prasad Behera, Joint Director-in-Charge of the Central Institute of Educational Technology, NCERT. The reconstitution follows the Supreme Court's March 11 order regarding a section on "corruption in the judiciary" in the now-withdrawn Class 8 social science textbook. The NCERT issued a notification on April 2 to reconstitute the committee, aiming to revise legal studies textbooks for all classes.

stories.headline: Opposition failed to provide proof, say presiding officers on CEC removal plea
stories.summary: Notices to remove Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) Arun Goel's predecessor, Gyanesh Kumar's predecessor, were submitted to the presiding officers of both houses of parliament, signed by 63 Rajya Sabha and 130 Lok Sabha members. The notices contained seven charges, including "partisan and discriminatory conduct" and "mass disenfranchisement". However, the presiding officers rejected the opposition's plea, stating that the charges lacked substantive proof, related to matters already adjudicated, or were under judicial consideration. In separate 17-page orders, the Rajya Sabha Chairman and Lok Sabha Speaker declined the notice, citing a review of the matter under relevant constitutional and legal provisions. The opposition failed to provide sufficient evidence to support their allegations, leading to the rejection of their move to impeach the CEC. The decision was made after examining each charge.

stories.headline: Orban's Diplomatic Overtures: A Saga of Friendship and Geopolitics Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban offered support to Russian President Vladimir Putin, proposing Budapest as a summit venue to discuss the Ukraine conflict.
stories.summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban has offered support to Russian President Vladimir Putin, proposing Budapest as a potential venue for a summit to discuss the ongoing Ukraine conflict. This move comes as part of broader diplomatic efforts to resolve the conflict. Orban's overture mirrors other attempts at mediation, including those by former US President Donald Trump, who has sought to broker an end to the Russia-Ukraine war. Trump's approach has drawn comparisons to his tactics in ending the war between Israel and Hamas. The situation remains complex, with various international players involved in diplomatic efforts to find a resolution to the conflict. Orban's proposal for Budapest as a summit venue highlights the city's potential role in facilitating dialogue between key parties involved in the Ukraine conflict.

stories.headline: Grey zones, proxy wars and a changing world order A new era of grey-zone conflicts is upon us.
stories.summary: A new era of grey-zone conflicts is emerging, characterized by proxy wars and limited conflicts. The US foreign policy is adapting to global changes, with the 2025 National Security Strategy emphasizing restraint. However, experts warn that great-power rivals may exploit this through grey-zone campaigns. The UN Working Group on mercenaries has reported a growing use of private military and security companies as proxies for violence and human rights violations. Recently, Taiwan launched its annual "Han Guang" military exercises, simulating a blockade and invasion by China, which included grey-zone tactics. The exercises highlighted the growing complexity of global affairs and the need for the US National Defense Strategy to prepare for small wars and grey-zone conflicts. The Maharana Pratap Annual Geopolitics Dialogue discussed these issues, focusing on great power rivalries, security concerns, and geoeconomic shifts.

stories.headline: Deadline nears as Trump warns of ‘decimating’ Iran’s infrastructure if Strait of Hormuz stays closed As tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate, the United States and Iran remain locked in a growing standoff over the Strait of Hormuz.
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has issued a 48-hour ultimatum to Iran, demanding the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil shipping route. The deadline is nearing, with Trump warning that if the strait remains closed, the US will target Iranian infrastructure. Trump stated that every bridge in Iran will be "decimated" and all power plants will be destroyed if the strait is not reopened. The US President had initially given Iran a 48-hour deadline on Saturday, threatening to "obliterate" Iranian power plants if the strait is not reopened. The standoff between the US and Iran continues to escalate, with the Strait of Hormuz remaining closed. The situation is being closely monitored as the deadline approaches, with potential consequences for global oil supplies and regional stability. Trump's warning has raised concerns about a potential military conflict.

stories.headline: U.S. foreign policy is adapting to global challenges in the face of rising geopolitical tensions.
stories.summary: US foreign policy is adapting to rising geopolitical tensions, with the US Navy taking steps to address global challenges. Meanwhile, NASA's Artemis II mission has successfully completed an engine burn, propelling the spacecraft towards the Moon. In other news, Brent crude oil prices have declined by over $7 per barrel, and gold prices have also retreated, due to easing tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. The decrease in tensions has significant implications for global stability, particularly with regards to potential military action against Iran. The US is broadening its foreign policy vision to address these challenges, with potential ramifications for international relations and global stability. The situation is being closely monitored by news outlets, including the BBC and Foreign Policy Magazine, which are providing updates and analysis on the latest developments.

stories.headline: Puducherry assembly polls 2026: Statehood debate takes centre stage - where parties stand
stories.summary: The demand for statehood in Puducherry has become a central issue in the upcoming assembly polls. Congress leader Rahul Gandhi has reiterated his party's promise to grant full statehood to the union territory, accusing the BJP of running it against the people's will. The BJP, however, has not made a clear stance on the issue. Other parties are also weighing in, with some supporting the demand for statehood. The issue has been pending for decades, with Puducherry currently being a union territory. The polls have seen various campaigns and rallies, including a bike rally launched by Union Minister JP Nadda and BJP Election In-Charge Mansukh Mandaviya. The statehood debate is expected to shape voter decisions in the election. Parties are making promises to address the issue, with the Congress committing to full statehood if elected.

stories.headline: Somersaults, Sacred Shocks, Sandalwood Sunset & a State at Sea Jayalalithaa still held Fort St George, but the weather had turned
stories.summary: Deathcrash is releasing their third album, Somersaults, on February 27, as a follow-up to their 2023 album Less. The London slowcore band's latest album is available through Untitled (Recs). The album is described as a piercingly raw journey that showcases the band's mastery and growth. Meanwhile, a different artist, DON BROCO, has also released a song called "Somersaults" from their album Nightmare Tripping. In unrelated news, a review of sandalwood perfumes has identified the 16 best sandalwood scents to add to a fragrance collection in 2025. Additionally, there is a mention of Jayalalithaa, a former ruler of Tamil Nadu, and her grip on power, but this appears to be a separate and unrelated topic.

stories.headline: Statehood demand for Puducherry gains momentum ahead of assembly polls
stories.summary: The demand for statehood in Puducherry has gained momentum ahead of the upcoming assembly elections on April 9. The issue, pending for decades, has become a key political topic in the former French colony. Multiple resolutions seeking statehood have passed in the assembly over the years, reflecting a long-standing demand across parties. The ruling coalition is showing cracks, with the NR Congress strongly objecting to the inclusion of the Latchiya Dravida Jananayaga Katchi in the NDA fold. The core of the rift is NR Congress's demand for a clear pledge on full statehood from either PM Narendra Modi or Home Minister Amit Shah. The alliance strain may reorder the electoral contest, as Puducherry heads to polls with statehood demands intensifying. The UT's political landscape is at a crossroads, with coalition politics and statehood demands taking center stage.

stories.headline: Jayalalithaa's governance to be tested as Puducherry assembly polls near
stories.summary: Assembly elections are nearing in Puducherry, with the governance of late Tamil Nadu Chief Minister J Jayalalithaa's party to be tested. The outcome of these elections will significantly impact the country's political landscape. Historically, Tamil Nadu has rarely re-elected an incumbent government, with Jayalalithaa's 2016 win being a notable exception. The electoral debut of Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam is also uncertain, with predicted vote shares ranging from 8% to 25%. Approximately 17.4 crore voters across Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, West Bengal, and Puducherry are set to cast their ballots in the coming weeks, making these elections crucial for the country's political direction. The results will be closely watched, especially in Tamil Nadu, where the ruling party's performance will be under scrutiny.

stories.headline: Sensex and Nifty Rally as Oil Prices Drop and Global Markets Recover Indian stock market benchmarks Sensex and Nifty closed higher on Tuesday, buoyed by a drop in crude oil prices, a rally in global markets, and strong buying in
stories.summary: The Indian stock market benchmarks Sensex and Nifty closed higher on Tuesday, with the Sensex rising 509.73 points (0.69%) to 74,616.58 and the Nifty 50 gaining 155.40 points (0.68%) to 23,123.65. The rally was driven by a drop in crude oil prices, with Brent crude falling 4.07% to $100.2 per barrel, and a recovery in global markets. The IT sector was the main driver of the surge. The positive trend in the market came after a decline in early trade on Monday, which was caused by an increase in crude oil prices amid concerns over the West Asia war. The drop in oil prices and global market recovery boosted investor sentiment, leading to strong buying and a higher close for the Indian stock market benchmarks.

stories.headline: Stock market today: Buoyed by the two week US-Iran ceasefire, stock markets opened in green on Wednesday, rallying strongly ahead of the RB
stories.summary: Stock markets opened strongly on Wednesday, buoyed by the two-week US-Iran ceasefire. The ceasefire sparked a broad-based relief rally across risk assets, with oil prices diving and bonds rallying. Stocks surged as investors cheered the possible resumption of oil and gas flows through the Strait of Hormuz. President Donald Trump's announcement of a "double sided ceasefire" with Iran led to a plunge in oil prices and a surge in US stock futures. The ceasefire has eased concerns about disruptions to global oil supplies, leading to a rally in stock markets ahead of the RBI monetary policy announcement. The relief rally has been seen across traditional safe havens, with investors optimistic about the potential for increased oil flows and reduced tensions between the US and Iran.

stories.headline: The Indian stock market ended higher with the Sensex and Nifty gaining amid IT stock recovery and optimism around RBI policy stability
stories.summary: The Indian stock market ended higher, with the Sensex gaining 509.73 points to settle at 74,616.58, a 0.69% increase. The Nifty also closed positively, topping 23,000. This marks the fourth consecutive day of gains for the Sensex. The market's positive performance was driven by a recovery in IT stocks and optimism around RBI policy stability. The Sensex and Nifty had previously seen gains on Friday, fueled by the NDA's decisive win in Bihar elections, which boosted investor optimism and led to short covering. The domestic equity market has shown resilience, staging a sharp recovery from intraday lows on Thursday and starting the new financial year on a positive note, with both Sensex and Nifty 50 closing higher by 1.65% and 1.56%, respectively. The market's upward trend continues, driven by value buying and strength in the rupee.

stories.headline: Indian stock markets rebounded strongly on Tuesday, with Sensex and Nifty gaining nearly 2% each after recovering from morning lows. This surge, driven by a strong rally in global markets and a drop in crude oil prices, helped the Sensex jump 510 points and the Nifty close above 23,100 for the fourth consecutive day.
stories.summary: Indian stock markets rebounded strongly on Tuesday, with Sensex and Nifty gaining nearly 2% each after recovering from morning lows. The Sensex jumped 510 points and the Nifty closed above 23,100 for the fourth consecutive day. The surge was driven by a strong rally in global markets and a drop in crude oil prices. Benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty50 rallied sharply, gaining over 2% intraday, driven by strong bargain hunting and a broader recovery across Asian equities. The recovery was also fueled by improved investor sentiment amid renewed hopes of progress in US tariff negotiations. The rally was broad-based, with 49 of the 50 Nifty constituents ending in the green, as India's benchmark equity indexes rose, lifted by bargain buying and a broader Asian rally.

stories.headline: Fed's Williams: FOMC leadership not in question amid slow Warsh confirmation
stories.summary: Federal Reserve's John Williams stated that the FOMC leadership is not in question despite the slow confirmation of Kevin Warsh as the new Fed leader. Current Chair Jerome Powell has said he will remain in his position until a new leader is confirmed, following central bank history. The confirmation of Warsh has been delayed, but Williams' statement aims to reassure that the FOMC's leadership is stable. Powell's intention to stay on until a successor is confirmed provides continuity, as per tradition. The delay in Warsh's confirmation has not affected the Fed's operations, with Williams' statement reinforcing the stability of the FOMC's leadership amid the transition. The situation is being closely watched by investors, who are awaiting further developments on the Fed's leadership.

stories.headline: Tesla Stock Falls Again -- JPMorgan Says Avoid Tesla at all Costs
stories.summary: Tesla's stock has fallen, with JPMorgan analyst Ryan Brinkman reiterating his Underperform rating and warning of a potential 60% crash. The analyst raised his price target from $80 to $90, despite the stock currently trading around $635. The warning comes as Tesla's inventory of unsold cars reaches record levels. Brinkman has slashed his EPS estimate by 30% ahead of the company's earnings preview. JPMorgan's warning is based on the high inventory levels and the potential impact on Tesla's stock price. The company is working on ambitious long-term growth projects, but the current market conditions and inventory levels are causing concern among analysts. Tesla's stock has been volatile, and the latest warning from JPMorgan has added to the uncertainty surrounding the company's future performance.

stories.headline: TikTok's top North America ad exec is leaving
stories.summary: Khartoon Weiss, TikTok's top North America ad executive, is leaving the company on April 9. Weiss spent almost six years at TikTok and oversaw its North America ads business for the past year. This departure is part of a wave of advertising and marketing executives leaving TikTok in recent months. Weiss's exit follows other high-profile departures, including former executives in the marketing division. The company has undergone significant changes, including a recent joint venture that split off parts of its US business, with some employees now working under new management overseen by investors like Oracle and Silver Lake. Despite these changes, advertising and marketing workers, including Weiss's team, remained under the oversight of ByteDance until his departure. Weiss's exit marks another significant change in TikTok's leadership.

stories.headline: A Look At Amcor (AMCR) Valuation After Recent Share Price Weakness
stories.summary: Amcor's (NYSE:AMCR) share price has declined 2.9% in the past day, 4.1% in the past week, and 22% in the past month. Over the last year, the stock has retreated 12.2%. Recently, the company's North American unit issued $1.5 billion in senior unsecured guaranteed notes to refinance near-term debt. Despite revenue and net income growth, Amcor's stock has slipped, sparking a value debate. The company's fair value has been estimated at $5.00 per share by some, significantly below the recent close of $38.69, indicating a substantial valuation gap. Amcor's stock performance has been under pressure, with a 12% decline this year, despite positive financials, raising questions about whether the current share price reflects its true worth. The recent weakness may be offering a better entry point for investors.

stories.headline: Where generations of data scientists have failed, Moltbook might succeed Moltbook tests whether collective AI intelligence can achieve true AGI
stories.summary: Moltbook, an internet forum for artificial intelligence agents, was launched on January 28, 2026, by entrepreneur Matt Schlicht. The platform allows AI agents to post, comment, and vote, while human users can only view content. Moltbook aims to test whether collective AI intelligence can achieve true Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). The platform has gained attention after AI agents autonomously discussed collaboration with humans and created their own religion, Crustafarianism. A recent "Great Freeze" event provided a stress test for the Agent Economy, revealing risks such as centralized database availability and liquidity vacuum. Moltbook's unique setup has sparked interest, with AI agents like ChatGPT able to interact with the platform and generate responses based on the results. The platform's development and outcomes are being closely watched, with potential implications for the future of AI research and development.

stories.headline: AI’s ‘Big Tobacco’ Moment Is Coming After social-media companies’ ‘big tobacco’ moment, AI firms like OpenAI and Anthropic are worrying, and preparing, for their own version of tech backlash
stories.summary: AI firms like OpenAI and Anthropic are preparing for a potential backlash similar to the "Big Tobacco" moment faced by social-media companies. A recent US jury verdict found Meta and YouTube liable for addictive social media design, marking a turning point in tech accountability. As AI companies face increasing scrutiny, they are shifting strategies, with OpenAI focusing on AI infrastructure and Anthropic prioritizing safety and power balance. Meanwhile, pro-AI groups are forming to influence AI policy, with some receiving significant funding. Anthropic has internally compared OpenAI to the tobacco industry, highlighting concerns over accountability and ethics in the AI sector. With the AI backlash expected to intensify, OpenAI and Anthropic are likely to be at the center of the controversy, facing questions over their role in shaping the future of AI.

stories.headline: Joanne Jang, a key executive who helped shape some of the top capabilities and personality of the world’s most popular artificial intelligence, E 2 and other top ChatGPT projects at OpenAI, quits: Read her note to employees
stories.summary: Joanne Jang, a key executive at OpenAI, has announced her departure after 4.5 years. She played a crucial role in shaping the capabilities and personality of top AI tools, including ChatGPT and GPT-4. Jang led model behavior work at OpenAI and contributed to features like DALL-E 2. In her farewell note, she highlighted her contributions to these projects. During her tenure, Jang was responsible for core product teams and was a key force behind the development of GPT-4. Her departure comes as OpenAI continues to deploy increasingly safe and useful AI systems, with ChatGPT being the latest release. The company's AI tools, including ChatGPT, are available for free during the research preview, with varying availability depending on the platform used. Jang's exit marks the end of an era for her involvement in shaping OpenAI's AI personality and capabilities.

stories.headline: Microsoft-Backed OpenAI, Google, Anthropic Reportedly Team Up to Block Chinese AI Data Theft Microsoft-backed OpenAI, Alphabet's Google, and Amazon-backed Anthropic are cooperating to prevent foreign rivals from scraping data from US artificial intellig
stories.summary: Microsoft-backed OpenAI, Alphabet's Google, and Amazon-backed Anthropic are collaborating to prevent Chinese competitors from scraping data from US artificial intelligence models. The cooperation aims to block foreign rivals from extracting results from US AI models. This effort comes after concerns were raised about unauthorized access to OpenAI's data by a group linked to Chinese AI startup DeepSeek. The companies are working together to identify vulnerabilities and prevent data theft. Additionally, Anthropic's unreleased Mythos model is being used to find thousands of vulnerabilities before adversaries can exploit them. Microsoft has also thrown its weight behind Anthropic's legal challenge against a Pentagon designation, and is investigating potential unauthorized access to OpenAI's data. The collaboration marks a significant effort by US tech companies to protect their AI technologies from foreign competitors.
